news.headline: GeneDx (NASDAQ:WGS) Downgraded by Zacks Research to "Strong Sell"
news.summary: Zacks Research cut its rating on GeneDx from Hold to Strong Sell, citing continued risk around execution and the fallout from the Q1 2026 Fabric Genomics impairment and guidance cut. The downgrade added to a mixed analyst picture even as several other firms had recently raised price targets.

news.headline: Guggenheim Raises GeneDx (WGS) Price Target, Maintaining a Buy Rating
news.summary: Guggenheim raised its price target on GeneDx to $74 from $70 while reiterating a Buy rating, reflecting incrementally improved sentiment following the stock's rebound from its May lows. The move added to a string of upward price-target revisions in late June.

news.headline: Piper Sandler raises GeneDx stock price target on coverage policy
news.summary: Piper Sandler lifted its GeneDx price target to $63 from $50 (Overweight maintained) after Carelon, the largest U.S. lab benefit manager, updated its clinical guidelines on June 14 to cover outpatient whole genome sequencing under the same criteria as exome testing. Analysts said the policy change, which brings genome-sequencing coverage to roughly 80% of insured patients, could add about $100 in average selling price by the second half of 2027 by supporting the exome-to-genome mix shift.

news.headline: GeneDx Holdings shareholders approve director election and other proposals at annual meeting
news.summary: At its 2026 annual meeting, GeneDx shareholders re-elected CEO Katherine Stueland as a Class II director, ratified Ernst & Young as auditor, and approved executive compensation on an advisory basis, with a strong preference for annual say-on-pay votes going forward.

news.headline: GeneDx Names Mark Gardner President to Lead Its Next Phase of Scale and Growth
news.summary: GeneDx appointed former Quest Diagnostics molecular genomics/oncology leader Mark Gardner as President, effective June 15, 2026, to oversee lab and commercial operations and report to CEO Katherine Stueland. Bryan Dechairo shifts to lead product, technology and innovation as the company looks to shore up execution after its Q1 stumble.

news.headline: GeneDx to Participate in the Goldman Sachs 47th Annual Global Healthcare Conference
news.summary: GeneDx management held a fireside chat at the Goldman Sachs 47th Annual Global Healthcare Conference in Miami on June 9, 2026, part of the company's push to rebuild investor confidence after May's guidance cut. A live and archived webcast was made available via the company's investor relations site.

catalysts.label: Q2 2026 earnings report
catalysts.detail: GeneDx is expected to report second-quarter 2026 financial results and provide an update on exome/genome volume growth and the reaffirmed/adjusted full-year 2026 revenue guidance of $475-490 million, following the steep cut disclosed in Q1. No exact date has been publicly confirmed yet.

catalysts.label: Carelon genome-sequencing coverage rollout
catalysts.detail: Carelon's June 14, 2026 clinical guideline update covering outpatient whole genome sequencing (now ~80% of insured lives) is expected by analysts (e.g., Piper Sandler) to gradually shift test mix toward genome sequencing and add roughly $100 in average selling price by the second half of 2027.

catalysts.label: WGSWW public warrant expiration
catalysts.detail: GeneDx's publicly traded warrants (NASDAQ: WGSWW) are scheduled to expire on July 22, 2026, a scheduled capital-structure event that could affect share count and dilution depending on exercise activity.

catalysts.label: ASHG 2026 Annual Meeting data presentations
catalysts.detail: GeneDx plans to showcase 14 research studies drawn from its GeneDx Infinity dataset (~1 million exomes/genomes) at the American Society of Human Genetics annual meeting, including newborn screening (GUARDIAN study) and neurodevelopmental disorder genetics findings.

catalysts.label: BEACONS multi-state newborn genomic screening enrollment
catalysts.detail: GeneDx is a sequencing partner in the NIH-funded BEACONS initiative and Florida's Sunshine Genetics Network, aiming to enroll up to 30,000 newborns across up to 10 states; continued enrollment and any new state partnerships could expand genome-sequencing volume.

pulse: GeneDx stock collapsed roughly 49% on May 5, 2026 after Q1 results revealed a $31.2 million non-cash impairment tied to the Fabric Genomics acquisition and a sharp cut to FY2026 revenue guidance (to $475-490M from $540-555M) driven by an adverse exome/genome product-mix shift; shares have since partially recovered, helped by heavy insider buying and the June 15 hire of new President Mark Gardner (ex-Quest Diagnostics) to tighten commercial execution. Sentiment has been further supported by Carelon's expanded outpatient whole-genome-sequencing coverage policy, which prompted Piper Sandler and Guggenheim to raise price targets on hopes of an ASP recovery, even as Zacks downgraded shares to Strong Sell on July 1. Core exome/genome testing volumes are still growing over 30% year-over-year, and the market now awaits Q2 2026 results for confirmation that volume growth is translating back into margin improvement.
